steps.title: Identify the unit rate
steps.content: We are given that the supply costs \$12.50 per pound. This is the unit rate, the cost per unit quantity.

steps.title: Calculate total cost by scaling the unit rate
steps.content:
To find the cost for 4 pounds, multiply the unit rate by the quantity:
$$12.50 \times 4 = 50$$
The cost for 4 pounds is \$50.00. Because the price is given per pound, the relationship is proportional: twice as many pounds would cost twice as much. No additional fixed fee is described, so multiplication alone gives the total.
